news.title: 2026 World Cup: All the European sides' fixtures and results
news.lede: Keep track of all the European sides' fixtures and results at the World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States. The 2026 FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States kicked off on 11 June and concludes with the final between Spain and holders Argentina in East Rutherford on 19 July. Check out all the details of the European teams' matches. Monday 15 June: D 0-0 vs Cabo Verde (Atlanta) Sunday 21 June: W 4-0 vs Saudi Arabia (Atlanta) Saturday 27 June: W 1-0 vs Uruguay (Guadalajara) Thursday 2 July (R32): W 3-0 vs Austria (Los Angeles) Monday 6 July (R16): W 1-0 vs Portugal (Dallas) Friday 10 July (QF): W 2-1 vs Belgium (Los Angeles) Tuesday 14 July (SF): W 2-0 vs France (Dallas) Sunday 19 July (F): vs Argentina (New York, 21:00).

news.title: How long was half-time at the World Cup final and did Fifa break the rules?
news.lede: The first World Cup half-time show led to a delay of almost half an hour. From reproductive rights to climate change to Big Tech, The Independent is on the ground when the story is developing. Whether it's investigating the financials of Elon Musk's pro-Trump PAC or producing our latest documentary, 'The A Word', which shines a light on the American women fighting for reproductive rights, we know how important it is to parse out the facts from the messaging. At such a critical moment in US history, we need reporters on the ground.

news.title: Melania Trump Sports American Brands for World Cup Finals Between Spain and Argentina
news.lede: While many of the fans at Sunday’s FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina at MetLife Stadium sported their favorite team’s colors, First Lady Melania Trump is showing off some American spirit. FLOTUS and President Donald Trump aren’t the only dignitaries in the estimated 80,000-person crowd in East Rutherford, N.J.. Spain’s Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez, Mexico’s President Claudia Sheinbaum and Canada’s Prime Minister Mark Carney were expected, as well as Spain’s King Felipe VI and Queen Letizia, who flew in with their daughters, Princess Leonor and Infanta Sofía. The Trumps were seated with FIFA president Gianni Infantino and his wife Leena Al Ashqar.

news.title: All The Celebrities Spotted At 2026 FIFA World Cup Final Match
news.lede: The 2026 FIFA World Cup Final at MetLife Stadium, pitting Argentina against Spain, transcended a mere sporting event, becoming a colossal entertainment spectacle. The tournament culminated with an unprecedented Halftime Show, curated by Chris Martin, featuring global music titans Madonna, Shakira, Justin Bieber, and BTS, all supporting the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund. The closing ceremony further amplified the star power with performances from Jennifer Hudson, Post Malone, and Robbie Williams. Off-stage, an extraordinary and diverse roster of celebrities, from Hollywood's Tom Cruise and Matt Damon to sports icons and political figures like Donald Trump, converged, solidifying the final as a monumental cultural fusion.

news.title: Ronaldo and Ronaldinho join Madonna in World Cup final half-time show performance
news.lede: Brazil legends Ronaldo and Ronaldinho joined Madonna in an extraordinary World Cup 2026 final half-time show. The Selecao icons were seen driving into the MetLife Stadium in New Jersey on a cart, with Madonna kicking off the show after Spain and Argentina played out a 0-0 draw in the first half. The two former Brazil players have been seen at various Fifa World Cup 2026 matches, even outside of their own nation’s games, with Carlo Ancelotti’s side exiting the tournament at the hands of Norway in the last 16. World Cup final live: Spain v Argentina, including half-time show updates.

news.title: England 2026 World Cup squad: player-by-player ratings
news.lede: After the team’s third-placed finish, we rate the 24 squad members who made an appearance at the tournament. Jordan Pickford Could he have done better with Enzo Fernández’s equaliser for Argentina? Jude Bellingham certainly thought so. But the man who has made more World Cup finals appearances than any other Englishman made some crucial saves, including a brilliant stop from Nicolas González in the semi-final.

news.title: Donald Trump arrives by helicopter to attend World Cup final alongside Gianni Infantino
news.lede: US president watches Spain v Argentina match. Trump avoids hostile reception from crowd. World Cup final: live coverage of the match. Donald Trump took his place at the World Cup final at the New York New Jersey Stadium on Sunday.

news.title: ‘Just stop’ – Tom Cruise’s ‘ridiculous’ pre-World Cup final speech leaves fans totally baffled
news.lede: WORLD CUP fans were left scratching their heads during Tom Cruise’s dramatic pre-final speech. Hollywood star Cruise, 64, talked about the planet coming together, from the middle of the MetLife Stadium pitch, before the ding-dong between Spain and Argentina. He began: “More than 30 days ago, 48 nations began a journey. They crossed oceans, they crossed borders, they crossed cultures.
